title: mentha requienii
intro: Mentha requienii, also known as Corsican mint, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It is known for its small, oval-shaped leaves and pinkish-white flowers, and is often used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Mentha requienii include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote healthy skin and respiratory function.
